title: Harper Fellowship 2026–2027 at The 19th for Emerging Journalists
summary: The Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 at The 19th is now accepting applications from emerging journalists interested in developing their careers in reporting, audience engagement, news product development, and nonprofit media leadership. The Harper Fellowship application deadline is March 20, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. PST, and the program offers a year-long salaried fellowship with full benefits and extensive professional development opportunities. About the Harper Fellowship Program The Harper Fellowship at The 19th is a prestigious journalism fellowship named after Frances Ellen Watkins Harper, widely recognized as the mother of African-American journalism. The program was launched in 2022 to create meaningful opportunities for graduates and alumni connected to Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) to gain experience in nonprofit journalism. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through the Harper Fellowship program, selected fellows spend a full year working with The 19th s editorial, product, audience engagement, and development teams. Fellows are embedded in their respective departments and contribute to meaningful journalism projects while gaining hands-on newsroom experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The Harper Fellowship focuses on strengthening diversity in journalism while providing fellows with mentorship, professional training, and career guidance that supports their long-term success in the media industry. Harper Fellowship Tracks and Learning Areas The Harper Fellowship program includes several fellowship tracks that allow participants to specialize in different areas of journalism and media operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The fellowship tracks include: Reporting Audience engagement News product Development (new track) Reporting Track Fellows in the reporting track gain experience covering stories through in-depth reporting and investigative work. Participants work closely with editors and journalists to produce original reporting that focuses on the intersection of gender, politics, and policy. Reporting fellows are encouraged to develop their own story ideas, collaborate with newsroom teams, and experiment with storytelling across digital platforms. Audience Engagement Track The audience engagement track focuses on helping fellows develop skills in digital storytelling and audience-centered journalism. Participants learn how to distribute journalism across multiple platforms such as websites, social media, newsletters, and search engines. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This track also involves creating engaging social media content and collaborating with editorial and creative teams to produce compelling headlines, visuals, and storytelling formats. News Product Track The Harper Fellowship news product track introduces fellows to product design and innovation in journalism. Participants learn how to develop features and digital tools that improve audience experience and support newsroom goals. Fellows work with product and technology teams to test ideas, analyze user feedback, and contribute to the development of journalism products such as newsletters, digital platforms, and audience engagement tools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Development Track The newly introduced Harper Fellowship development track focuses on the business and fundraising side of nonprofit journalism. Fellows gain hands-on experience in donor engagement, membership campaigns, and fundraising strategy. Participants also learn how nonprofit news organizations sustain independent journalism through diversified revenue models and community support. Benefits of the Harper Fellowship The Harper Fellowship provides a comprehensive support system designed to help fellows build long-term careers in journalism. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Key benefits include: A full-time salaried fellowship with benefits One year of mentorship and professional development Career coaching and job placement support Networking opportunities with media professionals Membership in the National Association of Black Journalists Attendance at the NABJ convention and career fair Access to journalism training workshops and seminars A professional development stipend A work-from-home stipend and wellness stipend An Apple MacBook laptop provided for the fellowship year These resources ensure fellows receive both financial and professional support throughout the program. Harper Fellowship Professional Development A major component of the Harper Fellowship program is its strong emphasis on professional growth. Fellows participate in training workshops and development sessions covering topics such as: Networking and career advancement Salary negotiation Resume and portfolio building Mental health and burnout prevention Financial literacy Media industry insights from experienced journalists The program also includes career coaching and mentorship to help fellows transition successfully into long-term journalism careers after completing the fellowship. Harper Fellowship Retreats and Networking The Harper Fellowship experience includes several retreats and networking events designed to connect fellows with media professionals and industry leaders. The fellowship year begins with an opening retreat that introduces new fellows to the program and provides opportunities to connect with previous fellowship cohorts. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Midway through the fellowship, participants attend a career development retreat focused on networking and preparing for future opportunities in journalism. Fellows also attend an annual staff retreat with The 19th team, allowing them to engage in strategic discussions, professional learning sessions, and collaborative activities. These events help fellows build meaningful professional relationships and expand their journalism networks. How to Apply for the Harper Fellowship Applicants interested in the Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 must submit an application through The 19th s official application platform. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The application typically requires: A cover letter explaining interest in the fellowship and career goals A resume outlining relevant experience Work samples demonstrating journalism or related skills Applicants should clearly describe how the Harper Fellowship aligns with their career aspirations and how they plan to contribute to public-service journalism. APPLY HERE Deadline Applications for the Harper Fellowship 2026 2027 must be submitted before the official deadline. Application Deadline: March 20, 2026 (5:00 p.m. PST) Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials early to avoid last-minute technical issues. Final Note The Harper Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for emerging journalists to gain hands-on newsroom experience while working alongside experienced media professionals. Through mentorship, professional training, and networking opportunities, the program equips fellows with the tools they need to build impactful careers in journalism. For individuals passionate about storytelling, public-service journalism, and media innovation, the Harper Fellowship can serve as a powerful launchpad for a long-term career in the industry. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); For more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and leadership programs, click here . title: World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea
summary: Overview Applications are now open for the World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 , a fully funded international programme organized within the framework of the UNESCO World Heritage Education Programme . Hosted by the Republic of Korea through the Korea Heritage Service (KHS) and the Korean National Commission for UNESCO (KNCU), the forum will take place from 13 21 July 2026 alongside the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Under the theme World Heritage, Communities and Education: Empowering Youth as Agents of Change, the programme will bring together 30 young professionals from around the world to strengthen their knowledge, skills, and engagement in the protection and management of natural and cultural World Heritage sites. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Forum Theme and Objectives The 2026 forum focuses on the relationship between World Heritage, communities, and education, with the goal of empowering youth to play an active role in heritage conservation and governance. Key objectives include: Strengthening the commitment and professional capacity of young practitioners working in heritage-related fields Exploring how community participation can improve conservation and management practices Examining the role of education, research, and learning in informed decision-making Promoting youth leadership and inclusive governance in World Heritage protection Through exchanges with local and international experts, participants will engage in collaborative learning and develop practical perspectives on sustainable heritage management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Programme Structure The forum combines academic discussions with practical, site-based learning experiences at World Heritage properties in the Republic of Korea. Activities include: Thematic presentations and expert-led discussions Collaborative workshops and group work Site visits and field-based learning Peer exchange with international participants Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum At the conclusion of the programme, participants will collectively prepare and present a Youth Declaration to the 48th Session of the World Heritage Committee. Provisional Schedule 12 July 2026: Arrival of participants 13 July 2026: Opening of the forum and introductions 14 19 July 2026: Thematic sessions, workshops, and site-based learning 18 July 2026: Joint session with the World Heritage Site Managers Forum 19 July 2026: Drafting of the Youth Declaration 20 July 2026: Finalisation and presentation of the Youth Declaration 21 July 2026: Closing session and departure Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Between 23 and 32 years old (born between 1994 and 2003 inclusive) Professional Profile Applicants should be young professionals or practitioners engaged in one or more of the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Education and teaching in formal learning settings World Heritage site management Research and innovation related to World Heritage NGOs or community-level associations focused on heritage Additional Requirements Demonstrated involvement in World Heritage-related activities Strong motivation to contribute to discussions on youth, communities, and education Full availability for the entire duration of the forum Proficiency in English (the working language of the programme) Selection Considerations The selection process aims to ensure: Balanced geographical representation Gender equality Diversity of professional backgrounds Potential for knowledge-sharing and impact after the forum Funding and Benefits The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 is fully funded. Selected participants will receive: International economy-class travel Accommodation Local transportation Meals during the programme Visa support and related fees where applicable Participants wishing to extend their stay after the programme must cover additional expenses independently. Application Process Interested candidates must apply through the official online application form: Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 Application Deadline: 15 March 2026 (23:59 CET) Important notes: Only complete applications submitted through the online form will be considered Selection is based solely on the information provided in the application Recommendations or endorsements are not required Selected applicants will be notified via email. Contact Information For questions regarding the application process: Email: For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org Why You Should Apply The World Heritage Young Professionals Forum is a flagship initiative of UNESCO s World Heritage Education Programme. It offers young professionals a unique opportunity to: Build international networks with peers and experts Gain practical experience through site-based learning Deepen understanding of the World Heritage Convention Contribute directly to global discussions on heritage conservation Strengthen leadership skills in community-based and sustainable heritage management For professionals working at the intersection of heritage, education, and community engagement, the forum provides both international exposure and long-term professional value. Call for Applications Selection of participants will be carried out solely on the basis of the online applications, in accordance with the criteria specified in the Call for Applications. Recommendations, nominations, or endorsements will therefore not be considered. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Online application Applications must be submitted through the online application form by 15 March 2026 at 23:59 CET Application Link: https://unesco-2023.limesurvey.net/662537 For any questions, kindly contact: wh-ypf@unesco.org For more info please visit: https://whc.unesco.org/en/news/2862/ The post World Heritage Young Professionals Forum 2026 (Fully Funded) — Call for Applications in South Korea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MENA Scholars Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Two-Year Research Fellowship for Early-Career Economists and Policy Researchers
summary: The third cohort of the MENA Scholars Fellowship (MSF) is now open for applications, offering a unique opportunity for early-career economists and researchers from the Middle East and North Africa to gain hands-on experience in rigorous impact evaluation and policy-relevant research. Launched by J-PAL Middle East and North Africa in partnership with Egypt Impact Lab , the fellowship is a prestigious two-year program designed to strengthen local research capacity and deepen evidence-based policymaking across the MENA region. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the MENA Scholars Fellowship The MENA Scholars Fellowship creates a structured pathway for researchers to design, implement, and contribute to randomized evaluations addressing poverty reduction and social development challenges. Fellows work closely with J-PAL-affiliated researchers, receive tailored mentorship, and participate in advanced training that blends academic rigor with real-world policy impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Each selected fellow receives a USD 15,000 stipend over the two-year period to support active participation in fellowship activities. What Fellows Will Gain Over the course of the fellowship, scholars will benefit from a comprehensive program that includes: Hands-on randomized evaluations alongside J-PAL affiliates, J-PAL MENA staff, and implementing partners One-on-one mentorship from senior researchers and scientific directors Access to regional and global research networks , including other Scholars Fellowship cohorts worldwide Advanced training , including completion of two MIT MicroMasters online courses focused on impact evaluation and econometric analysis ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Capacity-building workshops on proposal writing, technical research skills, and professional development Media and policy engagement support to amplify research findings Participation in regional policy events and presentations with leading academics An independent evaluation design project in the second year, guided by expert mentors Information Webinar To help prospective applicants better understand the fellowship, an information webinar will be held on Thursday, March 05, 2026, from 11:00 am to 12:00 pm Cairo local time . The session will cover eligibility requirements, the application process, and insights from previous fellows. Interested candidates can register via the official webinar registration form. After registration, participants will receive a Google Calendar invitation with the webinar link and event details. Application Timeline Application deadline: March 26, 2026 , at 11:59 pm Cairo Local Time Shortlisting notification: Mid-April 2026 Final interviews: Mid-May 2026 Fellowship start date: By June 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities here . Applicants must submit an online application form along with a resume and a motivation-focused cover letter. Shortlisted candidates will advance to a second application phase, which includes additional written questions and a short recorded video. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Funding and Institutional Support The fellowship is supported by Community Jameel and the Sawiris Foundation for Social Development , reinforcing a shared commitment to strengthening evidence-based policy research in the MENA region. For complete fellowship details, eligibility criteria, and access to the application form, applicants are encouraged to visit the official MENA Scholars Fellowship webpage. title: Sounddelivery Media Spokesperson Leadership Programme: Free 7-Month Training for Grassroots Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the Spokesperson Leadership Programme by Sounddelivery Media . The charity is seeking 14 grassroots leaders with lived experience of social injustice to take part in a fully funded, seven-month leadership and media development programme. About the Programme The Spokesperson Leadership Programme is designed to empower leaders directly affected by social injustice to confidently share their lived experiences and influence public conversations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Over seven months, selected participants will receive structured support to strengthen their leadership voice and media presence. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); What Participants Receive Professional training One-to-one mentoring Peer support network Media engagement opportunities Skills to influence public debate and drive change The programme focuses on equipping leaders with practical communication tools to engage media platforms and decision-makers effectively. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Who They re Looking For Grassroots leaders Individuals with lived experience of social injustice People committed to creating positive social change Those ready to engage with media and public platforms The programme is free of charge. Key Date to Remember Application Deadline: Friday 20th March, 12pm As spaces are limited to 14 participants, early application is strongly encouraged. title: George C. Marshall Fellowship 2026: Develop Grand Strategy Skills and Advance U.S. National Security Leadership
summary: Introduction: A Premier Fellowship in Grand Strategy and Strategic Leadership The George C. Marshall Fellowship is a prestigious leadership development program offered by The Heritage Foundation . Designed for exceptional conservative professionals early in their careers, this fellowship provides a comprehensive foundation in grand strategy theory and practice , strategic leadership, and the integration of national power instruments to advance U.S. national security interests. Inspired by the legacy of George C. Marshall , the program equips fellows with the intellectual framework and practical tools needed to address complex global challenges while preserving the principles of free enterprise, limited government, individual freedom, traditional American values, and a strong national defense. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The 2025 George C. Marshall Fellowship is structured as an immersive, multi-month experience combining expert-led discussions, hands-on workshops, historical analysis, and real-world strategic exercises. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the George C. Marshall Fellowship Mission The mission of the George C. Marshall Fellows program is to provide exceptional conservative professionals with the opportunity to: Apply the principles of grand strategy. Integrate all instruments of national power (diplomatic, informational, military, and economic). Develop practical approaches to achieving U.S. national security objectives. Strengthen leadership capabilities grounded in conservative policy principles. Vision The program offers a comprehensive overview of grand strategy theory and its practical application to today s global challenges. It is designed for individuals early in their careers who are likely to take on significant responsibilities in advancing U.S. national security. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants gain: Exposure to leading national security experts. Practical insights into defense and foreign policy. A structured understanding of geopolitical threats. Real-world exercises simulating strategic decision-making. The fellowship emphasizes both theoretical depth and applied leadership training , ensuring participants leave with actionable knowledge. 2026 George C. Marshall Fellowship Program Schedule The 2026 cohort follows a structured curriculum running from April through September. Program Launch April 30 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. This opening session introduces fellows to the program s intellectual framework and sets expectations for the months ahead. Defense Budget 101 May 14 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical briefing and workshop on the Defense Budget. This session provides an in-depth understanding of: The U.S. defense budgeting process. Strategic resource allocation. Budget priorities and trade-offs. How funding decisions reflect national strategy. Participants engage in hands-on analysis to connect policy objectives with fiscal realities. Gettysburg Staff Ride May 29 (All Day) Day trip, lunch, and battlefield tour in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania. This immersive historical experience at Gettysburg Battlefield allows fellows to: Study strategic decision-making in historical context. Analyze leadership under pressure. Evaluate operational successes and failures. Draw modern lessons from Civil War strategy. Staff rides are a traditional military leadership development tool, reinforcing critical thinking and situational analysis. Module 2: Deterring China June 11 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. June 25 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Practical exercise. This module focuses on: The strategic challenge posed by China. Deterrence theory and application. Military, economic, and diplomatic tools. Scenario-based strategic exercises. Fellows examine current geopolitical tensions and develop policy recommendations within a structured framework. Module 3: Hemispheric Security July 9 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and discussion with a guest speaker. July 23 (5 p.m. 7 p.m.) Dinner and panel discussion. This module addresses: Western Hemisphere security dynamics. U.S. relations with regional partners. Emerging threats in the Americas. Strategic coordination across institutions. Participants engage with policy experts and panelists to explore multidimensional security challenges. Program Graduation September 10 (Time and location TBD) Dinner and happy hour. The program concludes with a formal graduation event recognizing fellows achievements and fostering long-term professional networks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why Apply for the George C. Marshall Fellowship? The fellowship provides: Advanced training in grand strategy. Direct engagement with national security experts. Applied policy workshops. Leadership development grounded in conservative principles. Networking with peers committed to advancing U.S. national security. Exposure to real-world policy analysis and strategic planning. A prestigious credential associated with The Heritage Foundation. This is not merely a lecture series. It is an immersive strategic leadership development experience. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply through the official application portal. Marshall Fellowship 2026: Develop Grand Strategy Skills and Advance U.S. National Security Leadership appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
